Statement : Patna is 1200 km from Delhi and Shimla is 400 km from Delhi.
Conclusions:
I. Patna is 800 km away from Shimla.
II. Patna is 1600 km away from Shimla.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
The relative positions of the Patna and Shimla from Delhi is not given. Hence, the distance between them cannot be determined.

Q5 NAT Not checked
General Aptitude • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • Beginner • +1 / -0
Not attempted

The missing number of following the series is : 8, 9, 20, _______ 256, 1285.

NAT
Correct answer: 63 (63 - 63)
Solution

63 (63 - 63)

8, 8 × 1 + 1 = 9, 9 × 2 + 2 = 20, 20 × 3 + 3 = 63
63 × 4 × 4 = 256, 256 × 5 + 5 = 1285

DEXTROUS:ADROIT?

Correct answer: C
Solution

Correct answer: B
Solution

Q10 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • Beginner • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

A bag has a collection of 80 balls of 5 different colours: 24 Red, 23 Blue, 12 Green,10 yellow and 11 black. What is the minimum number of balls to be drawn so that we definitely have 14 balls of the same colour?

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Since the number of green, yellow and black balls is less than 14 each, we cannot have minimum 14 balls of any of these colours. In the worst-case scenario i.e. the number of balls that are drawn are 13-Red + 13-Blue + 12-Green + 10-Yellow + 11-Black = 59, we will not have at least 14 balls of the same colour. The next ball can be either Red or Blue which will lead to the required condition. Hence, a minimum of 60 balls have to be drawn to definitely have 14 balls of the same colour.

Q12 MCQ Not checked
General Aptitude • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • Beginner • +1 / -0.33
Not attempted

Identify the option which best fills in the blanks to make a meaningful sentence:
The task is made difficult by the fact that a/an ________ investigation of the improvement in energy, ________ and ______ effect has to face three conceptual problems yet to be fully explored.

Correct answer: D
Solution

(d)
Empirical investigation is based on conclusions that are exclusively derived from concrete and verifiable evidence. The rebound effect is the reduction in expected gains from new technologies that increase the efficiency of resource use. Hence, option (d) is most appropriate.

Q20 NAT Not checked
General Aptitude • MECHANICAL ENGINEERING • Beginner • +2 / -0
Not attempted

The number of 3 digit natural numbers which are neither multiples of 11 nor 13 are ___________.

NAT
Correct answer: 756 (756 - 756)
Solution

756 (756 - 756)

Smallest 3 digit multiple of 11 is 110 (=11 × 10)
Largest 3 digit multiple of 11 is 990 (= 11 × 90)
Giving us 90 – 10 + 1 = 81 three digit numbers which are multiples of 11.
Similarly, smallest 3 digit multiple of 13 is 104 (=13 × 8)
Largest 3 digit multiple of 11 is 988 (= 13 × 76)
Giving us 76 – 8 + 1 = 69 three digit numbers which are multiples of 13.
However, these 2 sets contain numbers which are multiples of 11 as well as 13 i.e. multiples of 11 × 13 = 143 (11 and 13 are co prime to each other). Number of 3 digit multiples of 143 are 143 × 1 = 143 to 143 × 6 = 858 i.e. 6 numbers which are multiples of 11 as well as 13.
Hence, the number of 3 digit numbers, which are multiples of 11 or 13 or both, are 81 + 69 – 6 = 144 which will give us the number of 3 digit numbers which are NOT multiples of 11 or 13 as 900 – 144 = 756.

(i) Channel C1 telecasts P5 on Sunday.
(ii) Channel C2 telecasts P3 on Tuesday.
(iii) Channel C3 telecasts P2 but not on Monday; it telecasts on the day next to that of C7 but on a day just prior to that of C4.
(iv) Channel C4 does not telecast P1 or P6 but telecasts on a day just prior to that of channel C6.
(v) Channel C7 telecasts P7 but not on Monday.
(vi) Program P1 is scheduled prior to program P6.
The program P4 is scheduled on _______ by channel ________.

Correct answer: B
Solution

(b)

No sentence has more than one error. When you find an error in any one of the parts (a), (b) or (c), indicate your response as (a) (b) or (c) option. You may feel that there is no error in a sentence. In that case, option (d) will signify a ‘No error’ response.
(ii) Errors may be in grammar, word usage or idioms. There may be a word missing or there may be a word which should be removed.

‘Either of which’, in general implies two choices, hence it would be better to say “any one of which”.
